paper_title: Plant Materials, Fungal Growth and Inoculation Methods

methods.description: Process for growing rice variety Huangxiuzan and maintaining optimal humidity conditions for fungal infection.

steps.description: Rice variety Huangxiuzan was grown in the experimental station of Plant Protection Institute, Guangdong Academy of Agricultural Science, Guangdong Province, China.
steps.searchable_summary: grow rice variety Huangxiuzan in controlled experimental station

steps.description: The experiment field was protected by a metal net, and there was an automatic water-spraying system underneath the net and above the field, this system sprayed mistlike water for 30 seconds per hour from 10 o'clock in the morning to 4 o'clock in the afternoon to increase humidity in the atmosphere during the infection period.
steps.searchable_summary: install and operate automatic water-spraying system to maintain humidity

methods.description: Method for culturing U. virens and inoculating rice plants to study fungal infection.

steps.description: PSB (potato and sucrose broth) medium was used to culture U. virens strain for inoculation.
steps.searchable_summary: culture U. virens in PSB medium

steps.description: U. virens was cultured at 28˚C for 10 days, the whole culture was stirred three times in a stirrer, each for 15 sec, to break down hyphae to small pieces.
steps.searchable_summary: incubate U. virens at 28˚C and stir to break down hyphae

steps.description: The resultant mixture of hyphae and spores were used to inoculate rice panicles.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare inoculum from hyphae and spores mixture

steps.description: We used a syringe with needle to inoculate rice plants at booting stage, each plant was injected with 2 -4 ml of inoculum until the liquid flooded out from the top of leaf sheath.
steps.searchable_summary: inject rice plants with inoculum using syringe

steps.description: Smut balls appeared in rice panicles about 20 days after inoculation.
steps.searchable_summary: observe smut balls formation in rice panicles
